overview.description: Display a list of items for user selection
overview.purpose: Display a list of items for user selection
whenToUse: When you need to present a list of choices to the user or allow to display navigation menus
whenNotToUse: When you have a single option or a different selection method.

examples.basic: <mc-list fit="medium" orientation="vertical" arialabel="Label" listsearchplaceholder="Search in the list" filtertype="contains"></mc-list>

advanced.LabelSublabelAsSlot: // Javascript import "@maersk-global/mds-components-core/mc-list"; import "@maersk-global/mds-components-core/mc-list-item"; // HTML Apple Fruit Apricot Fruit Artichoke Vegetable
advanced.WithGraphics: // Javascript import "@maersk-global/mds-components-core/mc-list"; import "@maersk-global/mds-components-core/mc-list-item"; // HTML ❤️ ✨ 🎉 🥰 🔥 😊 🥺 😂
advanced.WithGroupsAndDividers:
// JavaScript
import "@maersk-global/mds-components-core/mc-list";
import '@maersk-global/mds-components-core/mc-list-item';

// HTML
<mc-list>
  <small>Numbers</small>
  <mc-list-item value="1">One</mc-list-item>
  <mc-list-item value="2">Two</mc-list-item>
  <mc-list-item value="3">Three</mc-list-item>
  <hr />
  <small>Letters</small>
  <mc-list-item value="a">A</mc-list-item>
  <mc-list-item value="b">B</mc-list-item>
  <mc-list-item value="c">C</mc-list-item>
</mc-list>
advanced.WithSeparators:
// Javascript
import "@maersk-global/mds-components-core/mc-list";
import "@maersk-global/mds-components-core/mc-list-item";
    
// HTML
<mc-list>
  <mc-list-item label="Apples" sublabel="Fruits"></mc-list-item>
  <mc-list-item label="Apricots" sublabel="Fruits"></mc-list-item>
  <mc-list-item label="Artichokes" sublabel="Vegetables"></mc-list-item>
  <hr />
  <mc-list-item label="Carrots" sublabel="Vegetables"></mc-list-item>
  <hr />
  <mc-list-item label="Cherries" sublabel="Fruits"></mc-list-item>
  <mc-list-item label="Onions" sublabel="Vegetables"></mc-list-item>
</mc-list>
advanced.WithTooltip:
import "@maersk-global/mds-components-core/mc-button-group";
import "@maersk-global/mds-components-core/mc-button-group-item";

<mc-button-group>
  <mc-button-group-item value="Computer" trailingicon="computer">
    <router-link to="/computer">Computer</router-link>
  </mc-button-group-item>
  <mc-button-group-item value="Printer" icon="printer">
    <router-link to="/printer">Printer</router-link>  
  </mc-button-group-item>
  <mc-button-group-item value="File">
    <router-link to="/file">File</router-link>  
  </mc-button-group-item>
</mc-button-group>
advanced.WithActionsSlot:
// Javascript
import "@maersk-global/mds-components-core-list";
import "@maersk-global/mds-components-core-list-item";
import "@maersk-global/mds-components-core-menu";
import "@maersk-global/mds-components-core-button";

// HTML
<mc-list width="full-width" hasactionslots>
  <mc-list-item label="Invoice dispute | Booking BK-48217" sublabel="Finance · Last reply 2 minutes ago">
    <mc-menu slot="actions" trigger="click" role="listitem">
      <mc-button
        slot="trigger"
        icon="ellipsis-vertical"
        appearance="neutral"
        variant="plain"
        hiddenlabel
        padding="compact"
        label="Click to show menu"
      ></mc-button>
      <mc-list>
        <mc-list-item label="Open conversation"></mc-list-item>
        <mc-list-item label="Assign to team"></mc-list-item>
        <mc-list-item label="Archive thread"></mc-list-item>
        <mc-list-item label="Download transcript (PDF)"></mc-list-item>
      </mc-list>
    </mc-menu>
  </mc-list-item>
  <mc-list-item label="Vessel delay update | MV Aurora" sublabel="Export ops · Updated 45 minutes ago" disabled>
    <!-- No actions menu for disabled items -->
  </mc-list-item>
  <mc-list-item label="Customs clearance | HKG → LAX" sublabel="Customer chat · Yesterday 18:20">
    <mc-menu slot="actions" trigger="click" role="listitem">
      <mc-button
        slot="trigger"
        icon="ellipsis-vertical"
        appearance="neutral"
        variant="plain"
        hiddenlabel
        padding="compact"
        label="Click to show menu"
      ></mc-button>
      <mc-list>
        <mc-list-item label="Open conversation"></mc-list-item>
        <mc-list-item label="Assign to team"></mc-list-item>
        <mc-list-item label="Archive thread"></mc-list-item>
        <mc-list-item label="Download transcript (PDF)"></mc-list-item>
      </mc-list>
    </mc-menu>
  </mc-list-item>
</mc-list>

props.name: noborder
props.description: If exists, the border will be removed.

props.name: arialabel
props.description: Should be used to give a proper description to the list from accessibility point of view.

props.name: data
props.description: The data to be displayed in list. It is an array of objects `<IMcListDataItem>` that contains `{ label: string; value: string | number; disabled?: boolean }`

props.name: listsearch
props.description: If set to `true` or the attribute is just presented without any value, then the search box will be visible in the listbox, and user will be able to search and filter list items.

props.name: listsearchplaceholder
props.description: The placeholder text for the search input when listsearch is enabled.

props.name: filtertype
props.description:
Filter type to be used for filtering the list items. The default value is: `contains`, which will filter the list items based on any matching text within the item. 
The other option is: `startsWith`, which will filter the items that starts with the matching text.

props.name: customfilter
props.description: Specifies custom match function that should filter the results in the typeahead based on the typed text.

props.name: matchlabelonly
props.description: Specifies if internal or custom filter function should match and filter the results in the label text only based on the typed text. Match will not apply to sublabel or value of the data item

events.name: scroll
events.description: Dispatched when the list is scrolled.

events.name: listchange
events.description: Emitted when a list item is interacted.

events.name: listitemsloaded
events.description: Emitted when the list items are loaded.

events.name: focuschange
events.description: Emitted when a list item receives focus.

slots.description: Use the default slot, when you want to pass list items or dividers to the list. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.

cssParts.description: Using this CSS part you can change the look of the list wrapper inside the mc-list component. Check Examples -> Customised appearance story to see live example.
cssParts.name: list

guidelines.title: List
guidelines.description: Presents a list of selectable items to the user.

designPrinciples.title: Spacing
designPrinciples.description: Consistent spacing creates groups, rhythm and good scan-ability.

use-4-pixel-spacing: The base unit for spacing is 4 pixels, subsequent units are multiples of 4 (such as 8, 12, 16, etc.). These units should be used consistently for all paddings, margins, gutters, typography, and layouts to ensure a cohesive appearance and user experience across all Maersk digital products.
exceptions-to-4-pixel-spacing: When creating components, finer control is needed for spacing elements. Three acceptable exceptions here are 2, 6, and 10 pixels.
components: Relate the component elements and still keep them distinct by using space units dervided from the 4-pixel base spacing. These space units will make aligning and using non-core components with core ones easier.
patterns: A recognisable pattern is created when consistent spacing is used to establish a visual rhythm and relationship between the pattern elements.

Pattern spacing needs to be coherent which means not all space units need to be the same. Several spacing units can help establish better visual balance when dealing with differently-sized UI elements in the pattern.
layouts: Visual hierarchy in layouts requires well spaced regions and UI elements. Spacing units can control the information density of any application layout. Use them to provide the right level for the context and users' expectation.
information-density: Space is the element that controls the information density of any interface. Adjusting the space between blocks on information creates a visual rhythm and hierarchy.

High information density can be disorienting and overwhelming if spacing does not allow the eyes to rest and let people process what they see. People may notice the grouped elements but not process each individual item.

We recommend reading the <PageLink path="/design-language/component-fit/">Fit guidelines</PageLink> to help you make an informed decision what spacing units will be most suitable for your layout composition.

relatedGuidelines.title: Accessibility
relatedGuidelines.description: Improving the user experience for everyone.

what-is-accessibility?: Accessibility means that websites, tools and technologies are designed and developed in a way that makes it possible for people with disabilities to use them. Accessibility also benefits people without disabilities for example people with temporary disabilities or situational disabilities.
Accessibility helps people perceive, understand and interact with our digital products and it takes auditory, cognitive, neurological, physical, speech and visual limitations into consideration.

But accessibility is also a mindset and a change in the way we think and talk about digital products. It is a cultural change that cannot be made by one team only. We, therefore, urge all our colleagues as well as top management to get involved and share awareness about this very important topic. We need your support!
why-is-accessibility-important?: One of the main myths about accessibility is that it only concerns a small group of people with severe disabilities. This assumption is far from the reality and according to the World Health Organisation (WHO), more than 1 billion people are living with some level of impairment that affects the way they interact with digital products and access information online.
When our websites, apps and other digital products are designed badly, they create barriers that exclude people and make access to our offerings cumbersome and in some cases not possible at all.
being-inaccessible-is-a-problem: It is an ethical problem when we exclude certain groups of people from our products and services.

It is a legal problem, especially in the US where companies are being sued if they are not accessible, which could have financial consequences in terms of fines as well as affecting the brand negatively.

It is a big business problem - if we choose to exclude a very big group of potential customers because they have a disability.
When we design and build products with accessibility in mind, we not only open up our products and experiences for more people, but our products start to become more human - in the sense that they reflect the diversity of society and become much more flexible and robust to accommodate many different needs and use cases.
